tool.name: Nsight Compute (ncu)
tool.note: default /usr/bin/ncu is 2022.4.1.0 which does NOT support Blackwell (sm_120); must invoke 2025.2.1 explicitly. Profiling counter access required sudo (ERR_NVGPUCTRPERM bypass).

compute_workload_analysis.rule_finding: HighPipeUtilization OPT — all compute pipelines under-utilized (kernel is memory-bound). Estimated local speedup if compute-fixed: 94.19%

memory_workload_analysis.note: Memory Throughput ~516 GB/s, Max Bandwidth 78% — consistent with vector-add streaming pattern. Low L2 hit rate expected (each element touched once).

occupancy.rule_finding: AchievedOccupancy OPT — 22% gap from theoretical due to warp scheduling/load imbalance. Estimated local speedup: 21.99%

host_measured_bandwidth.ncu_overhead_note: Host-program bandwidth_GB_per_s during profiling = 0.7191 (profiling overhead, not representative). Native run measured 557.45 GB/s.

verdict: PASS — Nsight Compute profile run succeeded on RTX 5070 (sm_120) via sudo + 2025.2.1 ncu. All requested per-kernel metrics (SM throughput, DRAM bandwidth, register pressure, occupancy, achieved occupancy, L1/L2 hit rate) captured.
